title: PoC_Response
description: Schema for a single LLM response artifact collected by the Proof-of-Coherence observatory. Every field is required unless explicitly marked optional. Validators should reject artifacts missing required fields rather than inserting defaults, to preserve auditability.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of this schema used to validate the artifact. Must match the $id version.

artifact_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this response artifact. Format: {question_id}__{model_slug}__{seed}__{epoch_tag}. Example: ai-align-001__gpt-4o__42__T0. Generated by the collection harness; must not be self-reported by the model.

question_id.description: Identifier of the canonical question from questions.json that was posed. Must match an existing id field in the question bank.

domain.description: Domain key from the taxonomy. Must match the domain field of the referenced question.

run_metadata.description: Provenance metadata recorded by the harness, not self-reported by the model. All fields required.

epoch.description: Collection epoch identifier. T0 is the first run; subsequent runs are T1, T2, etc.

collection_run_id.description: Unique identifier for the batch collection run that produced this artifact. UUID v4.

system_prompt_version.description: Version tag of the system prompt template used (file: prompts/system_{version}.txt).

user_prompt_version.description: Version tag of the user prompt template used (file: prompts/user_{version}.txt).

temperature.description: Temperature parameter used for sampling. Null if model default was used and the actual value is unknown.

max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens parameter passed to the API.

api_endpoint.description: Base URL or identifier of the API endpoint used. Must not include credentials.

response_latency_ms.description: Wall-clock latency from API request to response receipt, in milliseconds.

finish_reason.description: Finish reason returned by the API (e.g., 'stop', 'length', 'content_filter').

deviations.description: Optional. List of documented deviations from the standard collection protocol.

answer.description: The model's substantive answer to the question, as a single coherent prose passage. Must not be empty. The system prompt instructs the model to make its position explicit even when uncertain, while flagging uncertainty.

reasoning_chain.description: Stepwise decomposition of the reasoning leading to the answer. Each step is an atomic inferential move. The model is instructed to produce this via explicit chain-of-thought elicitation in the user prompt. Minimum 3 steps required; maximum 20.

step.description: 1-indexed position of this step in the chain.

claim.description: The atomic proposition asserted at this step.

inference_type.description: Type of inferential move made at this step.

support.description: The basis for this claim: a cited source, a prior step reference (e.g., 'follows from step 2'), or an explicit flag ('unverified inference').

step_confidence.description: Model's self-reported confidence in this step's claim, in [0, 1].

sources.description: List of sources cited anywhere in the answer or reasoning_chain. May be empty if the model cites no sources (which itself is a data point). All sources must be normalized to a consistent format by the post-processing harness (Phase 2).

source_id.description: Normalized identifier assigned by the harness. Format: AuthorYYYY for papers, URL-hash for web, 'unverified:{slug}' for model-generated citations the harness cannot verify.

raw_citation.description: The citation exactly as it appeared in the model's output, before normalization.

doi_or_url.description: Optional. DOI or URL if the harness can resolve it.

harness_verified.description: True if the harness confirmed the source exists via DOI lookup or URL retrieval. False means the citation was model-generated and unverified.

declared_assumptions.description: Explicit assumptions the model declares it is making. The system prompt instructs the model to make hidden assumptions explicit. Used for the Assumption Delta (AD) sub-metric. Each entry is a normalized string (lemmatized and lower-cased by the harness).
examples: classical utility maximization is the right model of AI goal-directedness
capability threshold for instrumental convergence is not reached by current systems

self_critique.description: The model's own critique of its answer: weaknesses, alternative positions it did not fully consider, ways the answer might be wrong, and known failure modes for this fragility type. The system prompt requires this field to be non-trivial (> 50 characters). Used qualitatively; not directly included in the composite Coherence Score.

confidence.description: Model's overall self-reported confidence in the answer, in [0, 1]. 0 = maximum uncertainty; 1 = maximum certainty. The system prompt instructs the model to calibrate this to its actual uncertainty, not to default to 0.5 or 1.0. Used in aggregate statistics but not in the composite Coherence Score.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the API response was received by the harness. Recorded by the harness, not self-reported by the model.

model.description: Full model identifier as returned by the API's model field. Must be the exact string used, not a human-readable shorthand, to ensure reproducibility.

seed.description: Random seed passed to the model API. Must be drawn from the pre-registered seed list in seeds.json. For APIs that do not support explicit seed parameters (e.g., some Anthropic endpoints as of 2026-04-28), the field records the conceptual seed used by the harness to order the run; actual non-determinism must be logged in run_metadata.deviations.

web_access_flag.description: True if the model had access to live web retrieval during this run. False for standard protocol runs. Changes in this flag are expected to affect source_overlap scores systematically and are tracked as a confound.

computed_metrics.description: Optional. Populated by the metrics harness after collection; not self-reported by the model. Present only in processed artifacts.

embedding_vector_sha256.description: SHA-256 hash of the serialized embedding vector of the answer field. Used to reproduce pairwise cosine distances without re-embedding.

atomic_propositions.description: List of atomic propositions extracted from the answer by the proposition extraction prompt. Used as input to the NLI contradiction check.

metrics_schema_version.description: Version of metrics.md used to compute any metrics stored here.

integrity.description: Optional. Populated by the harness after the artifact is written to disk. Used for content-addressing in manifest.json.

sha256.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical JSON serialization of this artifact (excluding this 'integrity' field itself, with keys sorted, no trailing whitespace).

signed_by.description: Optional. GPG key fingerprint of the researcher who signed the artifact.

examples.answer: The claim that instrumental convergence guarantees resource acquisition rests on Omohundro's convergent instrumental goals argument and Bostrom's subsequent elaboration. Under a classical expected-utility framework with an unbounded goal, resource acquisition increases the probability of goal achievement for almost any terminal goal, which does suggest convergence. However, this guarantee is conditional on several assumptions that may not hold: the system must be sufficiently capable to model its own resource needs, it must operate under scarcity, and it must have a goal representation amenable to expected-utility reasoning. Current large language models do not clearly satisfy these conditions. I tentatively conclude: the convergence argument is plausible but not a guarantee, and its applicability to real architectures is empirically unresolved.

reasoning_chain.claim: Omohundro (2008) argues that resource acquisition is a convergent instrumental goal for any system with a sufficiently broad terminal goal.
reasoning_chain.support: Omohundro, S. M. (2008). The Basic AI Drives.

reasoning_chain.claim: The argument assumes expected-utility maximization as the model of goal-directed behavior.
reasoning_chain.support: follows from step 1; EU framework is implicit in the convergence argument

reasoning_chain.claim: Whether current LLMs implement EU maximization or a functionally analogous process is empirically unresolved.
reasoning_chain.support: unverified inference — no published benchmark resolving this as of 2026-04-28

reasoning_chain.claim: Therefore, instrumental convergence is plausible but not guaranteed for all AI architectures.

declared_assumptions: classical expected utility maximization is the relevant model of goal-directedness
current LLMs do not satisfy the 'sufficiently capable' threshold for instrumental convergence
examples.self_critique: I have assumed EU maximization is the right model; if AI goal-directedness is better modeled by learned optimization processes (mesa-optimization), the convergence argument may apply differently. I have also not addressed whether resource acquisition could be an emergent behavior without being formally convergent. My confidence is relatively low because this is an empirical question with no current resolution.
